# Code RED source/decompiled export
# Source: docs/code-red-script-workflow/ACTOR_TRAVEL_MENU_PLAN.md
# SHA1: a542bb739381981ccf35991ddf540bf1705e8379

# Actor Travel Menu Plan

This plan keeps actor travel work out of compiler confusion and inside the verified trainer/menu lane.

## Goal

Create a Code RED menu path that can command a selected actor to move to a chosen location without guessing native signatures.

Target command shape:

```text
select actor -> choose destination -> command actor -> log result
```

## Phase 1: debug foundation

Build these first:

1. Show player coordinates.
2. Save current player location to slot.
3. Load/list saved location slots.
4. Write a proof log.

Why: actor travel needs reliable coordinates before it needs actor pathing.

## Phase 2: actor selection foundation

Build these next:

1. Select nearest actor.
2. Show selected actor handle.
3. Show actor model/name if a verified function exists.
4. Clear selected actor.
5. Log invalid/dead/despawned actor handles safely.

Why: movement commands are unsafe until actor handles are proven.

## Phase 3: non-pathing movement proof

Build these before real travel:

1. Teleport selected actor to saved location.
2. Teleport selected actor to player.
3. Teleport player to saved location.

Why: this proves coordinate writes and selected actor control before task/pathing logic.

## Phase 4: travel/pathing proof

Only start this after a verified task/pathing function is found.

Possible menu commands:

```text
Travel to saved location
Travel to player waypoint
Travel to current player location
Follow player
Guard saved location
Stop current task
Regroup to player
```

Do not implement any of these until exact signatures are verified.

## Destination options

Safe order:

1. Current player position.
2. Saved location slot.
3. Manual coordinate entry.
4. Waypoint/map marker, only if a verified marker function exists.

## Logging requirements

Every command logs:

```json
{
  "feature": "actor_travel",
  "selected_actor": "handle or null",
  "destination_type": "player_position|saved_slot|manual|waypoint",
  "destination": {"x": 0.0, "y": 0.0, "z": 0.0},
  "command": "teleport|travel|follow|guard|stop",
  "result": "queued|success|failed|blocked",
  "errors": []
}
```

## Menu layout draft

```text
Code RED Actor Tools
  Selection
    Select nearest actor
    Clear selected actor
    Show selected actor info

  Locations
    Show player coordinates
    Save current location slot
    Load location slot
    Manual coordinate entry

  Movement
    Teleport selected actor to slot
    Teleport selected actor to player
    Travel selected actor to slot
    Follow player
    Guard slot
    Stop actor task

  Safety
    Respawn if dead [later]
    Dismiss actor [later]
    Log last command
```

## Do not add yet

- posse respawn loops
- waypoint travel
- combat escort AI
- horse/vehicle mounting
- full companion persistence

Those come after basic coordinate, handle, and task proof.
